questions.question: What is the significance of the EASe-GAD Trial in nutritional psychiatry?

options.text: It was the first study to prove that supplements cure anxiety
It was the first randomized controlled trial specifically testing dietary intervention for generalized anxiety disorder
It proved that Mediterranean diets prevent all mental health conditions
It was a large-scale study of magnesium supplementation for panic disorder

questions.explanation: The EASe-GAD Trial (2023) was the first rigorously designed RCT to test whether a structured dietary intervention could reduce anxiety symptoms in individuals diagnosed with GAD. Prior to this trial, virtually all nutritional psychiatry RCTs focused on depression.

questions.question: Which form of magnesium is preferred for anxiety management, and why?

options.text: Magnesium oxide, because it has the highest elemental magnesium content
Magnesium sulfate, because it is used in hospitals
Magnesium carbonate, because it is the most natural form
Magnesium glycinate, because it has ~80% bioavailability and glycine provides a synergistic calming effect

questions.explanation: Magnesium glycinate is the preferred form for anxiety. It has approximately 80% bioavailability, and glycine itself is an inhibitory neurotransmitter that provides a synergistic calming effect. By contrast, magnesium oxide has only about 4% bioavailability.

questions.question: At what dosage has inositol shown efficacy for panic disorder in clinical trials?

options.text: 500 mg to 1 g per day
2-4 g per day
12-18 g per day
50-100 mg per day

questions.explanation: Inositol at 12-18 g/day reduced panic attack frequency and severity in controlled trials. One crossover study found inositol comparable to fluvoxamine (an SSRI) in reducing panic attacks, with fewer side effects. The therapeutic dose is titrated up gradually from 2-4 g/day.

questions.question: How does reactive hypoglycemia relate to panic disorder?

options.text: It has no established relationship with panic disorder
It produces symptoms physiologically identical to panic — rapid heartbeat, sweating, tremor, dizziness, and dread
It only affects people with diabetes
It reduces anxiety by lowering cortisol levels

questions.explanation: Reactive hypoglycemia — the blood sugar crash that occurs 2-4 hours after high-glycemic meals — produces symptoms that are physiologically identical to panic. For individuals with panic disorder, these episodes can trigger or exacerbate panic attacks. Stabilizing blood sugar through balanced meals is a foundational intervention.

questions.question: Why was the systematic review finding that magnesium's anxiolytic effect is more pronounced in individuals with low baseline magnesium clinically important?

options.text: It means magnesium only works as a sedative at very high doses
It suggests the benefit is partly corrective, and given that 50-68% of the population may be magnesium-deficient, the practical relevance is substantial
It proves that magnesium works equally well for everyone regardless of baseline levels
It indicates that magnesium supplements should only be taken with a prescription

questions.explanation: The finding that magnesium supplementation for anxiety is most effective in individuals with low baseline status means the benefit is at least partly corrective (fixing a deficiency). Given that an estimated 50-68% of the general population consumes less than the RDA for magnesium, and stress itself increases magnesium excretion, the practical relevance is substantial.
